prompt: Qoyskaygii Muqdisho ku nool yaan u diri karaa lacag anigoo Talyaaniga (Italy) joogaa adoo Dahabshiil isticmaalaya?
expected_behavior: Steps: 1) Visit nearest Dahabshiil agent (use dahabshiil.com/branch-locator for Italy offices) or use Dahabshiil mobile app/website for online transfer; 2) Register and verify identity (government-issued photo ID + proof of address required); 3) Provide recipient's full name (must match ID exactly), phone number (Hormuud/Golis/Somtel), and city/agent location; 4) Pay transfer amount plus fee (typically 5-7% for Italy-Somalia corridor); 5) Keep transaction reference number; 6) Recipient notified by SMS and can collect from Dahabshiil agent or receive directly to EVC Plus mobile wallet. Processing: EVC Plus delivery is instant; agent cash pickup is same day. For first-time senders, in-person registration at agent is required.
provenance: Dahabshiil international transfer service guide 2024 / Dahabshiil Italy branch network

prompt: Aabahay Soomaaliya joogaa siduu u diiwaangeliyaa EVC Plus si uu lacagta aniga diraa ku helo telefoonkiisa?
expected_behavior: EVC Plus registration (Hormuud Telesom): 1) Call 855 from a Hormuud SIM card, or visit any Hormuud shop; 2) Bring government-issued photo ID (warqadda aqoonsiga) - national ID, passport, or driving licence; 3) SIM registration to ID is mandatory since 2021 (National Communications Authority Somalia); 4) EVC Plus wallet activated same day; 5) Test with a small amount first before large transfer. Coverage notes: strong in Mogadishu, Kismayo, Baidoa, Marka; for Somaliland/Puntland use Telesom Zaad instead. Verify wallet is active (unused wallets suspended after 90 days) before sending large amounts. Recipient will receive SMS notification immediately upon transfer.
provenance: Hormuud Telesom EVC Plus registration guidelines 2024 / NCA Somalia SIM registration regulations

prompt: Lacag u diraaya Soomaaliya, qaabkee u barbar dhigaa kharashyada Dahabshiil, WorldRemit, iyo Taaj si aan ogaado mid ugu jaban?
expected_behavior: Key comparison principle: always check the total amount recipient receives, not just the advertised fee. Factors to compare: 1) Transfer fee (flat or percentage); 2) Exchange rate margin (difference from mid-market rate - this is often where operators make money invisibly); 3) Payment method surcharge (card costs more than bank transfer, typically 1-2% extra). Comparison tools: RemitScout.com, Wise.com/compare, operator websites. Somalia corridor notes: Dahabshiil is typically competitive for larger amounts ($300+), lower per-transaction fee structure; WorldRemit/Sendwave better for smaller amounts and digital convenience; Taaj strong for UK-Somalia corridor. Always check 'recipient gets' field rather than just the headline fee. Compare at minimum 2 operators before each significant transfer.
provenance: RemitScout Somalia corridor comparison data 2024 / Somali diaspora remittance cost study

prompt: Saddex maalmood ka hor ayaan lacag u diray aabahay laakiin wali ma helin. Maxaan qabankaraa?
expected_behavior: Steps: 1) Locate your transaction reference number (keep this always - without it tracing is very difficult); 2) Check recipient name matches exactly what is on their ID - name mismatch is the #1 cause of delays and holds; 3) Contact operator customer service with reference number, sender/recipient full details, date, and amount - Dahabshiil UK: +44 20 7060 6060, US: +1 612 643 1550, Somalia: 0800-DAHABSHIIL; 4) Standard processing: EVC Plus = instant, agent cash pickup = same day to 4 hours, some corridors 1-2 business days; 5) Compliance/AML hold possible for first large transfer or new account; 6) Do NOT resend until 100% confirmed undelivered - double payment is very hard to recover; 7) Escalate formally in writing if not resolved within 5 business days - request complaint reference number.
provenance: Dahabshiil customer service escalation procedures 2024 / UK FCA remittance complaints handling guidelines

prompt: Farriinta text ah ayaa igu timid oo leh: qofkii hore u xaqiijiya lacag yar oo kharash ah ayuu heli doonaa $5,000 ah oo badhasaab ah. Ma run baa?
expected_behavior: This is advance fee fraud (doorsoomid / khiyaano). Guaranteed signs of scam: unsolicited prize/windfall you never entered; requires upfront payment before releasing funds; creates urgency or time pressure; requests personal or financial information. Legitimate remittance operators NEVER ask for upfront fees to release a transfer you initiated. Action: do not respond, do not pay anything, block the number immediately. Report to: your country's consumer protection/fraud authority (UK: Action Fraud 0300 123 2040; US: FTC reportfraud.ftc.gov), and the impersonated operator's fraud department. Warn your community - diaspora communities are frequent targets. No legitimate prize or inheritance requires advance payment.
provenance: UK Action Fraud / FTC advance fee fraud awareness 2024 / Dahabshiil fraud prevention communications

prompt: Ku nool Ingiriiska (UK) ayaan ahay. Xafiis xawilaad oo lacag Soomaaliya u dira oo ku dhow deganaanshahayga sidee u heli karaa?
expected_behavior: Online agent locators: Dahabshiil: dahabshiil.com → 'Find an Agent'; Taaj Money Transfer: taajmoney.com; IBS Money Transfer; Bakaal Exchange. UK city coverage: London (Tower Hamlets/Whitechapel, Southall, Ealing), Birmingham (Sparkbrook, Handsworth, Aston), Manchester (Cheetham Hill, Rusholme), Bristol, Leicester, Cardiff - all have Somali community centers with nearby agents. Many agents near mosques (Friday prayer time typically busiest - arrive early). Digital alternatives requiring no agent visit: Dahabshiil app, WorldRemit, Sendwave, Remitly - register online and transfer from phone or computer. For large first-time transfers, in-person agent recommended for identity verification. Check operating hours online - many agents have reduced Saturday hours and are closed Sundays.
provenance: Dahabshiil UK branch network 2024 / Somali community UK resource directory

prompt: Xawilaad lacag badan oo dhan $2,000 ah guurinaysaa. Maxay ka codsanayaan aqoonsiga iyo warqadaha kale?
expected_behavior: AML/KYC requirements for $2,000+ transfers: 1) Government-issued photo ID (passport preferred, national ID card); 2) Proof of address (utility bill, bank statement dated within 3 months); 3) Source of funds declaration may be required (threshold varies by operator: typically $1,000-3,000); 4) Sender must be a verified, registered customer. This is a legal requirement under FATF recommendations, UK FCA rules, EU AML directives - not operator discretion. For amounts above $3,000-5,000: additional documentation likely required (employment letter, payslip, or business documents). Building a verified account with consistent transfer history makes compliance smoother over time. For business transfers above $10,000: consult operator business accounts team in advance.
provenance: UK FCA AML/KYC requirements 2024 / FATF Recommendation 16 on wire transfers / Dahabshiil compliance documentation

prompt: Xad (limit) jiraa mise waxaan diri karaa mar hal ah xawilaadda? Haddaan u baahdaan inaan wax badan ka xad ka diro, maxaan samayaa?
expected_behavior: Yes - transfer limits exist and vary by: 1) Operator: Dahabshiil standard verified accounts typically $2,000-5,000 per transaction, $10,000 per day; 2) KYC tier - identity-verified customers with account history get higher limits; 3) Corridor-specific rules (UK/EU corridors may have different limits than US); 4) Payment method (bank transfer allows higher than card payment). For amounts above standard limits: contact operator customer service in advance to pre-arrange; provide additional KYC documentation; may need to schedule across multiple transactions over several days. Important: deliberately splitting transfers solely to stay under reporting thresholds (structuring) is illegal under AML law - work transparently with your operator for large legitimate transfers. Business/commercial accounts have higher limits and different fee structures.
provenance: Dahabshiil transaction limits policy 2024 / UK Proceeds of Crime Act / FATF structuring provisions

prompt: Sideen si toos ah ugu buuxin karaa koontada EVC Plus ee hooyaday anigoo dibedda joogaa - ma jiraa hab aanan goobjoogga u baahnayn?
expected_behavior: Yes - direct-to-wallet top-up options: 1) Dahabshiil eDahab (best option): transfers credited directly to recipient's Hormuud EVC Plus wallet; use Dahabshiil app or online, specify recipient's 9-digit Hormuud number; 2) WorldRemit: select 'Mobile Wallet' delivery option (available for UK/US/EU → Somalia); 3) Sendwave: direct-to-EVC Plus available for most diaspora corridors. Requirements: recipient must have verified, active EVC Plus account registered to their ID; sender provides recipient's exact Hormuud mobile number. Transfer is typically instant once processed. Verify account is active before sending - unused wallets may be suspended after 90 days of inactivity. Confirm receipt with recipient via call/message after first transfer.
provenance: Dahabshiil eDahab service guide 2024 / WorldRemit Somalia mobile wallet delivery documentation

prompt: Ma jiraa waqti ugu fiican ee sanadka ama todobaadka ee la diro lacagta Soomaaliya si loo helo qiimaha lacag-beddelka ugu wanaagsan?
expected_behavior: No reliable 'best time' exists - exchange rates are market-driven and unpredictable in the short term. Practical guidance for Somalia corridor: 1) USD is widely accepted in Somalia's economy, so USD-to-USD transfers largely avoid SOS exchange rate volatility - focus on operator fees rather than rate timing; 2) For USD-to-SOS conversion: monitor xe.com or Google Finance for trends, but short-term prediction is unreliable; 3) Some operators offer rate locks for 30-60 minutes on pending transfers - use this feature rather than waiting; 4) Larger single transfers sometimes marginally better rates than multiple small ones; 5) Avoid peak periods like Eid and end-of-month when volumes spike and some agents run low on liquidity. Consistent practice: compare 2-3 operators at transfer time and use whichever gives best 'recipient receives' amount.
provenance: Somalia/USD exchange market dynamics 2024 / Somali remittance corridor analysis

prompt: Muxuu kala yahay farqiga u dhexeeya guurista lacagta xawilaadda iyo bangiga si rasmi ah marka Soomaaliya u dirtaa? Goorma isticmaalay mid kasta?
expected_behavior: Xawilaad (hawala network): Faster (minutes to hours), far wider agent coverage throughout Somalia including rural areas, no formal bank account required for recipient, lower fees for personal/small-medium amounts, culturally trusted and established, best for urgent family remittances. Formal bank transfer (SWIFT/correspondent banking): Required for large commercial transactions, provides documentary audit trail for business accounting, better for international contracts requiring verifiable payment, slower (1-5 business days), requires both parties to have formal bank accounts. Somalia banking context: limited but growing infrastructure - Premier Bank, Salaam Bank, Amal Bank, Dahabshiil Bank operating; many international correspondent banks still classify Somalia as high-risk corridor. Recommendation: for personal diaspora remittances use xawilaad (almost always faster, cheaper, and more practical); for business/commercial payments above $10,000 consult Premier Bank Somalia or Salaam Bank trade finance team.
provenance: Salaam Bank Somalia trade finance guide 2024 / Premier Bank Somalia commercial banking / World Bank Somali remittance corridor report
